RULING: SPECIALLY-APPEARING UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
Parties stipulated to hear all matters simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.) Moving party's request for Judicial Notice for Exhibits 2-11 is granted by the Court per Evidence Code 452(d). Court declines to take Judicial Notice of Exhibit 1. Order signed in chambers. The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modifed: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(a). Trial is March 24, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of the preference window is March 27, 2014. Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the motions in limine the court will take appropriate actions. Last day for hearing summary motions is March 19, 2014. Summary motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P. Plaintiff stipulates to waive the additional 2 days for E-service. Plaintiff's deposition is scheduled to commence on December 10, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. and must be concluded by Dec. 31, 2013. The CMO presumptive time to complete a deposition in preference case is in place. The time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 10, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 17, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on March 19, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 7, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 21, 2014. No later than December 13, 2013, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than December 13, 2013, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 29, 2014 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on March 24, 2014 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by March 20, 2014 at 2:00p.m. Plaintiff to return the Medicare Authorization Forms to Berry and Berry by December 31, 2013.
